summary: Hub Navigation Reorganization - All tests PASSED. Verified: 1) Bottom tab renamed from 'Menu' to 'Hub' with apps icon, 2) Login screen does NOT show 'Ask Jessi' bar (useSegments() fix working), 3) All 9 collapsible sections visible for super_admin (My Brand, My Tools, Campaigns, My Performance, Setup & Manage, Account Management, Internal Operations, Learning, Settings), 4) Section accordion expansion works correctly, 5) All expected menu items present in each section, 6) Navigation links route to correct pages.
critical_code_review_comments: _layout.tsx line 154: Bottom tab renamed to 'Hub' with 'apps' icon - VERIFIED
_layout.tsx lines 71-76: useSegments() used to hide Jessi bar on auth screens - WORKING
more.tsx lines 461-630: 9 sections defined with correct role-gating
Section 1 'My Brand' (lines 471-482): 6 items including Digital Card, Link Page, Showcase, Review Link, Templates, Card Templates
Section 2 'My Tools' (lines 487-495): 4 items - Touchpoints, Ask Jessi, AI Follow-ups, Team Chat
Section 3 'Campaigns' (lines 500-509): 5 items - SMS/Email Campaigns, Dashboard, Broadcast, Date Triggers
Section 4 'My Performance' (lines 514-523): 5 items - My Stats, Customer Engagement, Leaderboard, Activity Reports, Email Analytics
Section 5 'Setup & Manage' (lines 529-544): Admin-only section with Store Profile, Brand Kit, Messaging Channels, Review Links, Tags, Team Members, etc.
Section 6 'Account Management' (lines 550-569): super_admin/partner only - Onboarding Hub, Account Health, Admin Dashboard, Organizations, Accounts, All Users
Section 7 'Internal Operations' (lines 574-596): super_admin only - Partner Portal, Partner Agreements, White Label, Billing, Phone Assignments
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hub navigation reorganization complete. The More screen now shows 9 collapsible accordion sections. For super_admin, all 9 sections are visible. For regular salesperson, only sections 1-4 + Learning + Settings would show. 'My Brand' section has defaultExpanded: true. Ask Jessi bar is hidden on auth screens using useSegments() from expo-router to detect 'auth', 'index', and 'onboarding' routes.
